generic_rules: Repairs should focus on fixing AGREE syntax errors without altering the underlying AADL architecture.
Use only identifiers (features, properties) visible in the AADL model when constructing AGREE contracts.
Ensure helper declarations (eq, const) have correct types (int, real, bool) matching the expressions they define.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed within `annex agree { ... };` blocks.
Use `eq` to define helper functions and `const` to define constants within the AGREE annex.
Range checks are commonly expressed as `bool = value >= lower_limit and value <= upper_limit;`
anti_patterns: Do not introduce new identifiers (types, properties) into AGREE that are not present in the AADL model.
Avoid placing guarantee statements within component implementations; use assignments instead.
Do not attempt to repair by adding AADL architecture; focus solely on correcting AGREE contract syntax.
